relatedness_reasoning: Both files share core loan and customer identifiers (loanApplicationId/loan_id, customerId/customer_id), loan financial terms (loanAmount/principal_amount, loanTerm/loan_term_months, currency), and the credit system is the common provider that would logically generate a repayment schedule after approving a lending decision override.

mappings.reasoning: loanApplicationId from the override message becomes the loan_id in the repayment schedule after approval.
Direct match of customer identifier between inbound and outbound.
Customer name is not present in the inbound lending_decision_override message; likely sourced from a customer master system.
Inbound productType is 'MORTGAGE' but outbound enum shows 'Personal Loan'; semantic mismatch suggests transformation or different product classification.
loanAmount from inbound becomes principal_amount in the repayment schedule.
Interest rate is not provided in the inbound lending_decision_override message; must be sourced from loan product configuration or pricing engine.
loanTerm from inbound directly maps to loan_term_months in outbound.
Loan start date is not present in the inbound override message; likely set to current date or approval date at time of schedule generation.
Maturity date is calculated from start_date and loan_term_months, neither of which are directly available in inbound.
Currency code is directly copied from inbound loanDetails.currency.
Schedule version is a system-generated identifier not present in the inbound message.
Generated date likely corresponds to the inbound message timestamp or current system time when schedule is created.
Repayment frequency is not specified in the inbound override message; likely a default or product-based configuration.
Total repayments is derived from loan term in months assuming monthly repayment frequency.
Repayment array is algorithmically generated from principal amount and term using amortization logic not present in inbound.
Payment number is a sequential index generated during schedule construction.
Due dates are calculated from start date and payment frequency, neither directly available in inbound.
Principal payment per installment is calculated via amortization schedule, not provided in inbound.
Interest payment per installment is calculated via amortization schedule using interest rate not in inbound.
Total payment is sum of principal and interest per installment, derived from amortization logic.
Outstanding balance is calculated iteratively through amortization schedule.
Repayment status is system-generated at schedule creation time, not present in inbound.
Total amount payable is sum of all repayments, calculated from amortization schedule.
Total interest is derived from amortization schedule using interest rate not in inbound.
Amount paid to date is zero at schedule generation time; not sourced from inbound.
Next payment due date is the first installment due date, calculated from start date.
Next payment amount is the first installment total, calculated from amortization schedule.
Inbound destination is 'credit' system, which becomes the source_system for the outbound message.
Destination system 'Customer Services' is not present in inbound; it is the configured target for this outbound message.
Outbound message_id is a new system-generated identifier for the repayment schedule transmission.
Outbound timestamp likely corresponds to when the repayment schedule message is generated/transmitted.
